segments.text: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people plotted against Jesus to put Him to death.
And when they had bound Him, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
Then Judas, His betrayer, seeing that He had been condemned, was remorseful and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,
I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.
segments.speaker: Judas

segments.text: And they said,
What is that to us? You see to it!
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Elders

segments.text: Then he threw down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed, and went and hanged himself.
Now Jesus stood before the governor. And the governor asked Him, saying,
Are You the King of the Jews?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: It is as you say.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The governor answered and said to them,
Which of the two do you want me to release to you?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Barabbas!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?
segments.speaker: Pilate

segments.text: Let Him be crucified!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: that is,
My God, My God, why have You forsaken Me?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So when the centurion and those with him, who were guarding Jesus, saw the earthquake and the things that had happened, they feared greatly,
Truly this was the Son of God!
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: Sir, we remember, while He was still alive, how that deceiver said, 'After three days I will rise.'
segments.speaker: Chief Priests and Pharisees
